description: Split the deck builder AI into three distinct modes — Strategist (deck structure and narrative), Art Director (full creative control, default), and Page Builder (direct HTML editing). Each mode has its own tool set, system prompt, and model routing.

userStories.title: Mode type definitions and configuration registry
userStories.description: As a developer, I need AgentMode types, config registry, and intent detection so the frontend and backend can route to the correct mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/deck-builder/agent-modes.ts
Export AgentMode type: 'strategist' | 'art-director' | 'page-builder'
Export AgentModeConfig interface with: mode, label, description, model, icon, placeholder
Export AGENT_MODES record with configs for all three modes
Export detectModeIntent(message, currentMode) — regex-based, returns AgentMode | null
Strategist signals: restructure, reorder, add a slide, remove slide, deck flow, storyline, outline, build me a deck
Page Builder signals: change the font, make it Npx, edit the heading, fix the padding, move this div, change background to
Art Director signals: redesign, make it more, improve this, show me options
Returns null if message fits current mode
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Strategist system prompt
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a strategist-specific prompt that focuses on deck structure, narrative flow, and content strategy.
acceptanceCriteria: Create getStrategistPrompt function
Focus on structure, narrative, and copy
No design/HTML guidance
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Page Builder system prompt
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a page-builder-specific prompt that focuses on direct HTML/CSS manipulation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create getPageBuilderPrompt function
Focus on HTML editing, CSS properties, surgical changes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: patch_slide_html tool handler
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a tool that applies surgical CSS/HTML patches to slides without rewriting the whole thing.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement patch_slide_html in processToolCall
Supports: replace, setAttribute, setStyle, setText, remove actions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Mode-specific tool sets
userStories.description: As a developer, I need getModeTools(mode) to return the correct tool subset for each agent mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Add getModeTools(mode: AgentMode): Anthropic.Tool[] to agent-modes.ts
Import ORCHESTRATOR_TOOLS from orchestrator-tools.ts and filter by tool.name
Strategist: get_all_slides, add_slide, delete_slide, reorder_slides, restructure_deck, edit_slide (title/body only), generate_section, delegate_research, get_deck_approach, load_skill, think
Art Director: ALL ORCHESTRATOR_TOOLS (unchanged)
Page Builder: get_slide_html, set_slide_html, patch_slide_html, edit_slide (all fields), get_media_assets, get_site_media, delegate_research, think
For strategist edit_slide: clone the tool definition but restrict field enum to ['title', 'body']
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Mode-aware chat route
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the chat route to accept a mode parameter and adjust behavior per mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify POST /api/admin/deck-builder/[id]/chat to accept mode: AgentMode in request body (default 'art-director')
Import AgentMode, AGENT_MODES, getModeTools from agent-modes.ts
Based on mode: select system prompt, tools, model
Page Builder: inject full slide HTML into prompt context, skip QA pipeline entirely
Strategist: block design delegation (return error if delegate_design called)
Emit { type: 'mode_info', mode } stream event at start
Save mode in message metadata_json
Art Director mode: completely unchanged from current behavior
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Mode tab bar UI component
userStories.description: As a user, I need a tab bar in the chat panel to switch between Strategist, Art Director, and Page Builder modes.
acceptanceCriteria: Add mode state to ChatPanel.tsx: useState<AgentMode>("art-director")
Create ModeTabBar with three tabs: Strategy, Design, Build
Active tab: sage green border-bottom-2 + accent text. Inactive: muted text
Tabs: font-mono, text-[11px], compact height
Tab bar sits between the chat header and messages area
Clicking tab sets mode, does NOT clear chat history
On mode change, insert divider in messages
Pass mode to sendMessage so it is in the API POST body
Chat input placeholder changes per mode (from AGENT_MODES)
Import AgentMode, AGENT_MODES from agent-modes-client.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created agent-modes-client.ts as a client-safe subset to avoid importing server-only modules (fs) in client components.

userStories.title: Mode-specific suggestion chips
userStories.description: As a user, I want suggestion chips that update based on the active mode so they are contextually relevant.
acceptanceCriteria: Create SUGGESTION_CHIPS_BY_MODE config (Record<AgentMode, string[]>)
Strategist: Build a sales deck, Restructure the flow, Add a closing slide, Improve the narrative
Art Director: Redesign this slide, Make it more impactful, Add data visualization, Show me 3 options
Page Builder: Make heading bigger, Add video grid, Change background color, Fix the spacing
Replace existing hardcoded SUGGESTION_CHIPS with mode-aware version
Chips update immediately when tab clicked
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fine-tune button on QA results
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Fine-tune button on QA action buttons so I can quickly switch to Page Builder mode after a QA result.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Fine-tune button to QA action buttons (alongside Accept/Improve/Redo)
Button: lightning icon + Fine-tune label
Clicking: sets mode to page-builder, inserts mode-switch divider, focuses chat input
Appears for both pass and needs_work QA results
The mode tab bar reflects the change
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Natural language mode detection suggestion
userStories.description: As a user, I want the system to detect when my message better fits a different mode and suggest switching before sending.
acceptanceCriteria: In trySend wrapper, before sending, call detectModeIntent(message, mode)
If non-null, show suggestion chip between input and messages
Two buttons: Switch (changes mode + sends) and Keep (sends in current mode)
Chip is unobtrusive — small bar
Disappears after any action
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Playwright E2E test — mode switching flow
userStories.description: As QA, I need an end-to-end test verifying mode switching works correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/deck-agent-modes.spec.ts
Log in as admin (team@claru.ai / qweqwe123!)
Navigate to deck builder with template 54f7930f-cf81-4c0d-8f66-28ddaf93476a
Verify default mode is Design (tab highlighted with accent color)
Click Strategy tab, verify it becomes active
Verify suggestion chips change to strategy-related options
Click Build tab, verify it becomes active
Verify suggestion chips change to build-related options
Verify chat input placeholder changes
Click Design tab, verify mode switches back
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Dev server at http://localhost:3005. UI-only test, no API calls needed. Use webapp-testing skill.

userStories.title: Playwright E2E test — Page Builder speed
userStories.description: As QA, I need a test verifying Page Builder mode responds faster than Art Director.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/deck-page-builder-speed.spec.ts
Test requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (skip if not set)
Log in as admin, navigate to deck builder
Switch to Build mode, send a simple edit message
Assert response time < 10 seconds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Dev server at http://localhost:3005. Use webapp-testing skill.
